sql_injection.description: SQL Injection: User input is concatenated directly into a SQL query without sanitization.
sql_injection.remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.

xss.description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS): User input is injected into HTML without sanitization.
xss.remediation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize input with a library like DOMPurify.

command_injection.description: Command Injection: User input is passed to a system command without validation.
command_injection.remediation: Avoid using exec(), spawn(), or system() with user input. Use allowlists or escape shell arguments.

hardcoded_secret.description: Hardcoded Credentials: API keys, passwords, or tokens found in source code.
hardcoded_secret.remediation: Use environment variables or a secure secret management service. Never commit secrets to version control.

eval_injection.description: Insecure use of eval() with dynamic input.
eval_injection.remediation: Avoid eval(). Use safer alternatives like JSON.parse() or a dedicated parser.
